_metadata.description: Unicron Trilogy characters — Armada (2002-2003), Energon (2004-2005), Cybertron (2005-2006)

characters.name: Rad White
characters.notes: Primary human protagonist. Bradley 'Rad' White, a curious kid who accidentally awakens the Mini-Cons on Earth, triggering the Armada conflict. Partners with Mini-Con High Wire

characters.name: Carlos Lopez
characters.notes: Human ally. Rad's best friend who joins the Autobots' adventures. Partners with Mini-Con Grindor

characters.name: Alexis Thi Dang
characters.notes: Human ally. Intelligent and technically skilled friend of Rad and Carlos. Partners with Mini-Con Sureshock

characters.name: High Wire
characters.notes: Street Action Mini-Con Team member. De facto leader of the Mini-Cons. Partners with Rad. Combines with Grindor and Sureshock to form Perceptor. Known as Wheelie in Japan

characters.name: Grindor
characters.notes: Street Action Mini-Con Team member. Partners with Carlos. Combines with High Wire and Sureshock to form Perceptor

characters.name: Sureshock
characters.notes: Street Action Mini-Con Team member. Partners with Alexis. Combines with High Wire and Grindor to form Perceptor

characters.name: Perceptor
characters.notes: Combined form of the Street Action Mini-Con Team (High Wire, Grindor, Sureshock). Figurehead of the Mini-Con race. Possesses telepathic abilities

characters.name: Optimus Prime
characters.notes: Autobot leader. Can Powerlinx with Jetfire for flight capability and with Overload for shoulder-mounted cannons (Megaweapon mode). Known as Convoy in Japan

characters.name: Hot Shot
characters.notes: Young, impulsive Autobot with leadership potential. Trained by Scavenger. Mini-Con partner is Jolt. Known as Hot Rod in Japan

characters.name: Red Alert
characters.notes: Autobot medic and technical specialist. Mini-Con partner is Longarm. Known as Ratchet in Japan

characters.name: Smokescreen
characters.notes: Steadfast Autobot fighter. Critically damaged by Megatron and rebuilt as Hoist. Known as Grap in Japan

characters.name: Hoist
characters.notes: Smokescreen's rebuilt form after being critically damaged by Megatron. New body and identity. Known as Grap Super Mode in Japan

characters.name: Scavenger
characters.notes: Grizzled Autobot mentor and double agent. Trained Optimus Prime in combat. Initially poses as a Decepticon mercenary. Known as Devastar in Japan

characters.name: Blurr
characters.notes: Speed-focused Autobot. Lone wolf personality, initially reluctant to work with the team. Known as Silverbolt in Japan

characters.name: Side Swipe
characters.notes: Young Autobot warrior. Name also spelled Sideswipe on some releases. Known as Stepper in Japan

characters.name: Jetfire
characters.notes: Autobot second-in-command. Can Powerlinx with Optimus Prime to grant flight capability. Mini-Con partner is Comettor. Known as Skyfire in Japan

characters.name: Overload
characters.notes: Autobot powerhouse. Can combine with Optimus Prime to form Megaweapon mode with massive shoulder-mounted cannons. Known as Ultra Magnus in Japan

characters.name: Laserbeak
characters.notes: Autobot drone assigned to protect the human children. Given to Rad, Carlos, and Alexis by Red Alert. Not a Mini-Con

characters.name: Sparkplug
characters.notes: Optimus Prime's Mini-Con partner. Emerged as a leader among the Mini-Cons due to heroic actions. Known as Prime in Japan

characters.name: Jolt
characters.notes: Hot Shot's Mini-Con partner. More mature than Hot Shot, tries to teach him teamwork. Grants Hot Shot temporary flight when Powerlinxed

characters.name: Longarm
characters.notes: Red Alert's Mini-Con partner. Serves as Red Alert's second brain for analyzing technical problems. Saved from near-death and modified by Red Alert

characters.name: Comettor
characters.notes: Jetfire's Mini-Con partner. Adventurous explorer who transforms into a landing strut and cannon for Jetfire's shuttle mode

characters.name: Megatron
characters.notes: Decepticon leader. Upgrades to Galvatron mid-series. Can combine with Tidal Wave for enhanced power. Mini-Con partner is Leader-1. Known as Megatron in Japan

characters.name: Galvatron
characters.notes: Megatron's powered-up form in Armada. Same character, upgraded designation. Eventually allies with Autobots against Unicron. Known as Megatron Super Mode in Japan

characters.name: Starscream
characters.notes: Decepticon Air Commander and schemer. Unusually complex portrayal — defects to Autobots mid-series before returning to Decepticons. Sacrifices himself against Unicron. Mini-Con partner is Swindle

characters.name: Demolishor
characters.notes: Loyal Decepticon heavy-hitter. Follows Megatron without question. Mini-Con partner is Blackout. Known as Ironhide in Japan

characters.name: Cyclonus
characters.notes: Decepticon soldier. Chaotic and reckless fighter who enjoys aerial combat. Mini-Con partner is Crumplezone. Known as Sandstorm in Japan

characters.name: Thrust
characters.notes: Decepticon tactician and master manipulator. Later becomes an agent of Unicron after Sideways manipulates him. Mini-Con partner is Inferno. Known as Thrust in Japan

characters.name: Tidal Wave
characters.alt_mode: Triple warship (aircraft carrier, battleship, landing craft)
characters.notes: Massive Decepticon warship. His three vehicle components unite into one warship then convert to robot mode. Can combine with Megatron as armor. Known as Shockwave in Japan

characters.name: Wheeljack
characters.notes: Former Autobot turned Decepticon. Was Hot Shot's close friend before feeling abandoned on the battlefield and being rescued by Megatron. Driven by revenge. Known as Rampage in Japan

characters.name: Leader-1
characters.notes: Megatron's Mini-Con partner. Former Mini-Con leader captured and forced into servitude. Harbors desire for revenge against Megatron. Known as Barrel in Japan

characters.name: Swindle
characters.notes: Starscream's Mini-Con partner. Self-interested and persuasive. Given the nickname 'Grid' by Starscream. Known as Grid in Japan

characters.name: Blackout (Mini-Con)
characters.notes: Demolishor's Mini-Con partner. Free-spirited and independent, often clashes with Demolishor's obedient nature

characters.name: Crumplezone (Mini-Con)
characters.notes: Cyclonus's Mini-Con partner. Thrill-seeker who enjoys Cyclonus's chaotic flying style

characters.name: Inferno (Mini-Con)
characters.notes: Thrust's Mini-Con partner. Grows arrogant from the power of his partnership. Not to be confused with the G1 Autobot of the same name

characters.name: Jetstorm
characters.notes: Air Defense Mini-Con Team member. Combines with Runway and Sonar to form the Star Saber sword. Forms the lower blade

characters.name: Runway
characters.notes: Air Defense Mini-Con Team member. Combines with Jetstorm and Sonar to form the Star Saber sword. Forms the blade tip

characters.name: Sonar
characters.notes: Air Defense Mini-Con Team member. Combines with Jetstorm and Runway to form the Star Saber sword. Forms the hilt

characters.name: Star Saber
characters.notes: Combined weapon form of the Air Defense Mini-Con Team (Jetstorm, Runway, Sonar). Possibly the mightiest weapon known to Transformer-kind. The component Mini-Cons retain consciousness and can deactivate the sword in protest

characters.name: Dirt Boss (Mini-Con)
characters.notes: Race Mini-Con Team member. Combines with Downshift and Mirage to form the Skyboom Shield. Forms the middle portion

characters.name: Downshift (Mini-Con)
characters.notes: Race Mini-Con Team member. Combines with Dirt Boss and Mirage to form the Skyboom Shield. Forms the upper end

characters.name: Mirage (Mini-Con)
characters.notes: Race Mini-Con Team member. Combines with Dirt Boss and Downshift to form the Skyboom Shield. Forms the pointed end. Not to be confused with the G1 Autobot

characters.name: Skyboom Shield
characters.notes: Combined weapon form of the Race Mini-Con Team (Dirt Boss, Downshift, Mirage). The ultimate defensive force, capable of diffusing attacks from the Requiem Blaster

characters.name: Astroscope
characters.notes: Space Mini-Con Team member. Scientifically-minded. Combines with Payload and Sky Blast to form the Requiem Blaster. Forms the firing barrel

characters.name: Payload
characters.notes: Space Mini-Con Team member. Combines with Astroscope and Sky Blast to form the Requiem Blaster. Forms the handle and base

characters.name: Sky Blast
characters.notes: Space Mini-Con Team member. Combines with Astroscope and Payload to form the Requiem Blaster. Forms the main chamber

characters.name: Requiem Blaster
characters.notes: Combined weapon form of the Space Mini-Con Team (Astroscope, Payload, Sky Blast). Draws on supernova energy, quasar sonic output, and black hole gravity. Only the Skyboom Shield can diffuse its attack

characters.name: Sideways
characters.notes: Agent of Unicron. Manipulates both Autobots and Decepticons by switching faction allegiance. Mini-Con partners are Rook and Crosswise, who form his interchangeable heads

characters.name: Rook
characters.notes: Sideways' Mini-Con partner. Unicron-affiliated spy and saboteur. Functions as one of Sideways' interchangeable heads. Can combine with Crosswise to form Mirror

characters.name: Crosswise (Mini-Con)
characters.notes: Sideways' Mini-Con partner. Unicron-affiliated agent of chaos. Functions as one of Sideways' interchangeable heads. Can combine with Rook to form Mirror. Not to be confused with the G1 character

characters.name: Unicron
characters.notes: The Chaos Bringer. Planet-sized Transformer and ultimate antagonist of Armada. Creator of the Mini-Cons as instruments to fuel conflict. Autobots and Decepticons unite to defeat him in the series finale

characters.name: Nemesis Prime
characters.notes: Unicron-created dark counterpart of Optimus Prime. Scanned minds of Transformers and took Prime's form. Wields the Vorpal Saber. Mini-Con partner is Run-Over

characters.name: Run-Over
characters.notes: Nemesis Prime's Mini-Con partner. Created by Unicron as a near-mindless clone of Over-Run. Possesses trans-phase mode for teleportation. Known as Sweep in Japan

characters.name: Billy
characters.notes: Human ally. Former bully who becomes an ally of the Autobots after discovering their existence. Accompanies the team throughout the series including the final battle against Unicron

characters.name: Fred
characters.notes: Human ally. Billy's friend and comic relief character known for his constant snacking. Joins the Autobots' adventures and accompanies them to Cybertron

characters.name: Kicker Jones
characters.notes: Human protagonist of Energon. Full name Chad 'Kicker' Jones. Son of Dr. Brian Jones. Has ability to sense energon, granted by Primus. Partners with Ironhide. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Alpha Quintesson
characters.notes: Mysterious multi-faced Quintesson who commands the Terrorcons. Creator of Scorponok. Seeks to use Unicron's power to restore his destroyed homeworld Planet Q. Has four rotating face masks representing different personalities. Dies expending his energon to create a new world. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Ironhide
characters.notes: Young eager Autobot who partners with Kicker Jones. Idealistic and sometimes overzealous. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Scorponok
characters.notes: Commander of the Terrorcons, created by Alpha Q from the spark of a wandering Decepticon. Warrior of pride who favors direct combat. Later gains Hyper-Mode with energon-powered weaponry. NOT the same as any Armada character. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Wing Saber
characters.notes: Originally Wing Dagger, rebuilt by Primus into Wing Saber after being grievously wounded. Autobot security officer who can combine with Optimus Prime. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Omega Supreme
characters.notes: Ancient Autobot Guardian of Cybertron. Transforms into Omega Train and Omega Battleship. Can combine with Optimus Prime to form Optimus Supreme. Has a small robot partner Omega that becomes his head. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Rodimus
characters.notes: Disillusioned Autobot military commander who left Cybertron to rebuild rather than fight. Leads Team Rodimus. Believes Unicron can be used to restore lost worlds. Eventually cooperates with Optimus Prime. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Prowl
characters.notes: Logical Autobot who values analysis over rushing into combat. Member of Team Rodimus. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Landmine
characters.notes: Experienced Autobot warrior, Rodimus's right-hand soldier and bodyguard. Member of Team Rodimus. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Bulkhead
characters.notes: Elderly but capable Autobot aerial defense soldier. Leads Team Bulkhead. Impulsive and eager despite his age. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Cliffjumper
characters.notes: High-risk thrill-seeking Autobot rookie. Member of Team Bulkhead. Respects his instructor Bulkhead. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Downshift (Energon)
characters.notes: Former headstrong rookie who matured into a silent and cautious soldier under Hot Shot's training. Member of Team Bulkhead. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Inferno (Energon)
characters.notes: Autobot fire truck. New character for Energon, distinct from Armada Mini-Con Inferno. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Arcee
characters.notes: First female Omnicon. Powerful archer and leader of the Omnicons. Born when energon resonated with the Omnicons' desire for motivation. Has the strength of one hundred soldiers. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Signal Flare
characters.notes: Omnicon craftsman who excels at sculpting raw energon into weapons. Skilled at detecting and manipulating energon. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Skyblast
characters.notes: Young Omnicon and skilled tactician. Can convert raw energon into energon chips and weaponry. A bit immature despite his talents. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Strongarm
characters.notes: Omnicon who greatly prefers labor over combat. Works in energon mines converting raw energon into energon stars. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Shockblast
characters.notes: Arrogant and powerful Decepticon sociopath. Originally designed as Shockwave but renamed due to trademark issues. Kills indiscriminately and challenges even Megatron's authority. Older brother of Six Shot. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Six Shot
characters.notes: Younger brother of Shockblast. Decepticon mechanical engineer who prefers scheming and backstabbing over direct confrontation. Formidable in combat with impervious armor. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Mirage (Energon)
characters.notes: Skilled Decepticon warrior who destroys Autobots with artistic precision. High-speed gunboat effective on sea and land. One of Megatron's best warriors. Depicted as rebuilt Tidal Wave in cartoon but intended as new character by Hasbro. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Snow Cat
characters.notes: Decepticon warrior who can overcome any environment with insane fighting tactics. Treated as upgraded Cyclonus in cartoon but as a separate new character in toy bios and comics. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Battle Ravage
characters.notes: Merciless Terrorcon drone incapable of feeling emotion. Uses energon-enhanced jaws and mace-tail. Sometimes individual, often mass-produced. Gathers raw energon for Alpha Q. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Divebomb
characters.notes: Terrorcon drone with keen tactical caution. Wields razor-sharp energon blades that combine into a boomerang weapon. Mass-produced. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Cruellock
characters.notes: Formidable Terrorcon fighter with terrifying speed. Takes delight in suffering of enemies and comrades alike. Both individual and mass-produced drone species. Can handle raw energon. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Insecticon
characters.notes: Swarming energon-devouring Terrorcon killing machine. Semi-sentient drone, dull-witted but vicious and well-armed. Both individual and mass-produced species. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Constructicon Maximus
characters.notes: Combined form of the Construction Team (Steamhammer, Bonecrusher, Duststorm, Sledge, Wideload). Decepticon gestalt warrior. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Steamhammer
characters.notes: Skilled communications specialist and code breaker. Construction Team leader and torso of Constructicon Maximus. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Bonecrusher
characters.notes: Construction Team member and left leg of Constructicon Maximus. Rarely presented as sentient individual in cartoon. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Duststorm
characters.notes: Construction Team member and left arm of Constructicon Maximus. Rarely appeared as individual in cartoon. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Sledge
characters.notes: Construction Team member and right arm of Constructicon Maximus. Never individually named or presented as sentient in cartoon. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Wideload
characters.notes: Construction Team member and right leg of Constructicon Maximus. Ancient warrior from Cybertron's deep past. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Bruticus Maximus
characters.notes: Combined form of the Destruction Team (Barricade, Blackout, Blight, Kickback, Stormcloud). Decepticon gestalt warrior powered by super-energon. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Barricade
characters.notes: Leader of the Destruction Team and torso of Bruticus Maximus. Completely unarmed in robot mode. Super-energon-powered. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Blackout
characters.notes: Destruction Team member and left arm of Bruticus Maximus. Super-energon-powered military vehicle. Distinct from Armada Mini-Con Blackout. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Blight
characters.notes: Destruction Team member and right arm of Bruticus Maximus. Super-energon-powered. Never individually named or sentient in cartoon. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Kickback
characters.notes: Destruction Team member and left leg of Bruticus Maximus. Super-energon-powered. Never individually named or sentient in cartoon. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Stormcloud
characters.notes: Destruction Team member and right leg of Bruticus Maximus. Super-energon-powered. Never individually named or sentient in cartoon. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Superion Maximus
characters.notes: Combined form of the Air Team (Storm Jet, Sky Shadow, Terradive, Treadshot, Windrazor). Autobot gestalt warrior. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Storm Jet
characters.notes: Leader of the Air Team and torso of Superion Maximus. Brawler at heart, always itching for a fight. Expert marksman. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Sky Shadow
characters.notes: Air Team member and right leg of Superion Maximus. Never individually named or sentient in cartoon. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Terradive
characters.notes: Air Team member and left arm of Superion Maximus. Never individually named or sentient in cartoon. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Treadshot
characters.notes: Air Team member and right arm of Superion Maximus. Destroyed by Bruticus Maximus in series finale. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Windrazor
characters.notes: Air Team member and left leg of Superion Maximus. Never individually named or sentient in cartoon. Destroyed by Bruticus Maximus. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Dr. Brian Jones
characters.notes: Humanity's foremost energon expert. MIT graduate in physical engineering. Father of Kicker and Sally Jones, husband of Miranda. Develops energon towers and the Miranda II research vessel. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Misha Miramond
characters.notes: Human energon systems researcher and specialist. Kicker's romantic interest. Works closely with Autobots on energon applications. Becomes key crew member on Miranda II. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Miranda Jones
characters.notes: Wife of Dr. Brian Jones, mother of Kicker and Sally. Calm and gentle counterpoint to her husband's eccentricity. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Sally Jones
characters.notes: Younger sister of Kicker Jones. Headstrong and brave. Assists with Space Bridge operations and helps during battles on Cybertron. Debut: Energon

characters.name: Coby Hansen
characters.notes: Primary human protagonist, age 14. Mechanically gifted teen who repairs Landmine and becomes key Autobot ally. Can hear the Omega Frequency. Marries Lori and becomes an astronaut. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Bud Hansen
characters.notes: Coby's younger brother. Imaginative and enthusiastic, bonds closely with Mini-Con Jolt. Can hear the Omega Frequency. Later becomes an Oscar-winning film director. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Lori Jiménez
characters.notes: Human protagonist. Develops close bonds with Override ('Big Sister'/'Little Sister') and Scourge. Can hear the Omega Frequency. Marries Coby and becomes an astronaut. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Professor Lucy Suzuki
characters.notes: Scientist who theorizes Earth is hollow and inhabited by aliens. Eventually proven right and becomes Autobot ally. Assists with Omega Lock research. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Vector Prime
characters.notes: Ancient guardian of time and space, one of the original Thirteen created by Primus. Emerges from outside time to warn the Autobots about the Unicron Singularity and guide the search for Cyber Planet Keys. Sacrifices himself. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Override
characters.notes: Leader of Velocitron (Speed Planet). Best racer on her world. Initially cocky but matures through Autobot alliance. Close bond with Lori ('Big Sister'). Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Brakedown
characters.notes: Oldest living inhabitant of Velocitron. Acts like a doddering old fool but possesses extensive racing knowledge. Mentors Clocker and helps prepare Hot Shot for races. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Clocker
characters.notes: Young Velocitronian racer and Hot Shot admirer. Serves as guide for the Autobots on Velocitron. Participates in Planet Cup racing. Remains on Velocitron under Hot Shot's leadership. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Ransack
characters.notes: Velocitron Decepticon. The 'brains' of his duo with Crumplezone, though that is limited praise. A coward, sneak, and small-time schemer. Aspiring stand-up comedian. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Crumplezone
characters.notes: Velocitron Decepticon. The brawn of his duo with Ransack. Largest and possibly slowest Velocitronian, but still faster than most off-worlders. Not the sharpest tool. Debut: Cybertron. Not the Armada Mini-Con of the same name

characters.name: Dirt Boss
characters.notes: Velocitronian racer from the 'baja' sector. Initially a Decepticon, later works with Autobots. Obsessed with defeating Override. Startlingly fast despite small stature. Oversized clear tires. Debut: Cybertron. Not the Armada Mini-Con of the same name

characters.name: Buzzsaw
characters.notes: Old-school Decepticon. Serves as announcer and commentator for Velocitron's Planet Cup racing. Remained on Cybertron during the Unicron Singularity crisis. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Scourge
characters.notes: Leader of Jungle Planet (Animatron). Initially a tyrannical ruler who believes in rule through strength. Learns true strength from Lori and joins the Autobots. Decepticon turned Autobot. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Backstop
characters.notes: Wise elder of Jungle Planet (Animatron). Teaches peace and mentors Snarl. Attempted to educate a new generation in the ways of peace against Scourge's tyranny. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Snarl
characters.notes: Jungle Planet native. Leads Autobot-loyal Jungle Planet natives. Skilled, cunning, silent warrior trained by Backstop. Holds grudge against Scourge. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Undermine
characters.notes: Jungle Planet native. Silent spy, assassin, and bodyguard for Scourge. Hunts down dissidents. Decepticon turned Autobot. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Brimstone
characters.notes: Jungle Planet native. One of Scourge's right-claw mechs. Enjoys beating people up. Not particularly intelligent but loyal. Decepticon turned Autobot. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Metroplex
characters.notes: Head foreman of Gigantion (Giant Planet). Calm, quiet, intelligent giant. Wields the axe Sparkdrinker. Mini-Con partner is Drill Bit. Represents Gigantion at the interplanetary peace conference. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Quickmix
characters.notes: Metroplex's right-hand mech on Gigantion. Cautious yet brave, knows a lot about a lot. Helps convince Metroplex to violate tradition to protect their world. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Menasor
characters.notes: Gigantion worker who resents his planet's rigid social structure. Initially joins Megatron's Decepticons seeking respect, later joins the Autobots under Metroplex. Mini-Con partner is Heavy Load. Not a combiner despite sharing the G1 name. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Evac
characters.notes: Autobot guardian stationed on Earth for centuries to protect the Cyber Planet Key. Has used many disguises over the years including 14 different helicopters. Claims to have never been to Cybertron. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Crosswise
characters.notes: Autobot monster hunter stationed on Earth. Professional monster hunter with an array of weaponry. Descended from the crew of the Atlantis. Spent millennia battling Ancient Decepticons. Debut: Cybertron. Distinct from the Armada Mini-Con and RID character of the same name

characters.name: Thundercracker
characters.notes: Decepticon jet warrior. First appears ten years after the Energon War. Scanned a Su-35 jet for his Earth disguise. Distinct from the G1 character. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Thunderblast
characters.notes: Decepticon femme fatale. Obsessed with power and drawn to the strongest male Decepticons. Expert aquatic combatant. Arrived on Earth millions of years ago in the colony ship Atlantis, imprisoned until freed by Starscream. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Mudflap
characters.notes: Autobot with anger issues. Former boxing champion and friend of Landmine. Briefly defects to Decepticons under Starscream's manipulation before rejoining Autobots. Uses free-flying limbs fighting technique. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Lugnutz
characters.notes: Free-spirited wanderer and road scholar. Initially Decepticon but has no interest in conquest, just works for energon. Uses 1960s-influenced slang and writes poetry. Friends with Thunderblast. Later joins Autobots. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Soundwave
characters.notes: Planet X survivor. Information specialist who uses voice mimicry and sonic abilities. Seeks revenge on Gigantion for destroying Planet X. Works alongside Sideways and partner Laserbeak. Possibly Planet X's most intimidating warrior. Debut: Cybertron. Distinct from G1 and Armada characters of the same name

characters.name: Laserbeak (Cybertron)
characters.notes: Soundwave's drone partner from Planet X. Programming derived from Soundwave's personality. Stores in Soundwave's chest compartment. Equipped with stealth capabilities and cutting lasers. Despite toy packaging, NOT a Mini-Con. Debut: Cybertron. Distinct from the Armada Laserbeak

characters.name: Primus
characters.notes: Creator-god of the Transformers. Transforms into the planet Cybertron itself. Awakened during the Cybertron series to help combat the Unicron Singularity. Has both planet and robot modes. A multiversal force for good. Debut: Cybertron (first cartoon appearance in robot mode)

characters.name: Jolt (Cybertron)
characters.notes: Recon Mini-Con Team member from Gigantion. Biggest geek around, studies Earth culture. Has teleportation abilities activated by spinning his rotors. Bonds closely with Bud Hansen. Debut: Cybertron. Distinct from the Armada Mini-Con of the same name

characters.name: Reverb
characters.notes: Recon Mini-Con Team member. Most adventurous of the three but tends to get in over his head. Reconnaissance specialist assisting Vector Prime and the Autobots. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Six-Speed
characters.alt_mode: Toyota GT-One Le Mans-style race car
characters.notes: Recon Mini-Con Team member from Gigantion. Curious and fairly sensitive. Interested in understanding human behavior and customs. Reconnaissance specialist. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Drill Bit
characters.notes: Gigantion Mini-Con. Personal attendant to Metroplex. Most working Mini-Cons report to him instead of Metroplex directly. Has a caring spark but capable in combat. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Heavy Load
characters.notes: Menasor's Mini-Con partner and moral compass. Acts as Menasor's 'Jiminy Cricket', preventing reckless decisions. More level-headed than his larger partner. Shortest Mini-Con available. Debut: Cybertron

characters.name: Signal Lancer
characters.notes: Autobot stationed in the desert with a traffic light disguise suggested by Lori. Initially unnamed in English dub (named in Galaxy Force). Joins Metroplex's crew for space bridge construction. Debut: Cybertron
